verses.text: The whole congregation comes to the wilderness of Sin, six weeks after leaving Egypt. They murmur against Moses and Aaron: would we had died in Egypt, where we sat by flesh pots and ate bread to the full. You brought us into this wilderness to kill us with hunger. Guidance: A month out of slavery, and Egypt has already started to look like a buffet in memory. Hunger rewrites history quickly.

The whole congregation comes to the wilderness of Sin, six weeks after leaving Egypt. They murmur against Moses and Aaron: would we had died in Egypt, where we sat by flesh pots and ate bread to the full. You brought us into this wilderness to kill us with hunger. Guidance: A month out of slavery, and Egypt has already started to look like a buffet in memory. Hunger rewrites history quickly.

The LORD: I will rain bread from heaven. The people shall gather a portion every day, that I may prove them — whether they will walk in My law. On the sixth day they shall prepare twice as much. Guidance: Provision is also a test. God measures faithfulness by what we do with the daily gift — not by how desperately we want extra.

The LORD: I will rain bread from heaven. The people shall gather a portion every day, that I may prove them — whether they will walk in My law. On the sixth day they shall prepare twice as much. Guidance: Provision is also a test. God measures faithfulness by what we do with the daily gift — not by how desperately we want extra.

Moses and Aaron: at evening you shall know that the LORD brought you out of Egypt; in the morning you shall see His glory — for He hears your murmurings. What are we, that you murmur against us? Your murmurings are not against us, but against the LORD. Guidance: When God's people complain to leaders, they are often complaining past them. Pastors and parents are not the target; God hears the real address.

Your murmurings are not against us, but against the LORD. Guidance: When God's people complain to leaders, they are often complaining past them. Pastors and parents are not the target; God hears the real address.

Moses to Aaron: tell the congregation, come near before the LORD; He has heard your murmurings. As Aaron spoke, the glory of the LORD appeared in the cloud. The LORD: I have heard. At evening you shall eat flesh; in the morning, bread. You shall know that I am the LORD your God. Guidance: God answers complaint not with rebuke but with quail and bread. Sometimes He addresses the murmuring stomach before He addresses the heart.

At evening quails cover the camp; in the morning the dew lies around the host. When the dew is gone, a small round thing lies on the wilderness, fine as frost. They say to one another: what is it? — man hu, in Hebrew. Moses: this is the bread the LORD has given you. Guidance: The name of God's daily mercy is the question we ask of it — He gives you something that doesn't fit the categories you already have.

They say to one another: what is it? — man hu, in Hebrew. Moses: this is the bread the LORD has given you. Guidance: The name of God's daily mercy is the question we ask of it — He gives you something that doesn't fit the categories you already have.

Gather according to what each can eat — an omer per person, by the number in your tents. They did so; some gathered more, some less. When measured, the one with much had nothing over; the one with little had no lack. Every man gathered to his eating. Guidance: God's economy doesn't reward hoarding. The greedy had no surplus; the timid had no shortfall. Provision is sized to need.

Moses: let no man leave any of it till morning. But some did not listen and kept part of it; it bred worms and stank. Moses was angry. So they gathered it every morning, each according to his eating; when the sun grew hot, it melted. Guidance: God's provision is daily by design. Tomorrow's mercy will be there tomorrow; today's mercy spoils overnight. Trust is what gets you out of bed.

On the sixth day they gathered twice as much — two omers per person. The rulers told Moses. He said: this is what the LORD said — tomorrow is the rest of the holy sabbath. Bake what you will bake, boil what you will boil; lay up the rest for morning. Guidance: The sabbath is built into the manna pattern from the start. Rest is not added later; it is woven into how God feeds you.

On the sixth day they gathered twice as much — two omers per person. The rulers told Moses. He said: this is what the LORD said — tomorrow is the rest of the holy sabbath. Bake what you will bake, boil what you will boil; lay up the rest for morning. Guidance: The sabbath is built into the manna pattern from the start. Rest is not added later; it is woven into how God feeds you.

They laid it up till morning, as Moses commanded; it did not stink, no worm in it. Moses: eat that today, for today is a sabbath unto the LORD; today you shall not find it in the field. Six days you shall gather it; the seventh, sabbath, there is none. Guidance: The same manna that spoiled on weekdays kept clean on the eve of sabbath. God will preserve what He commands you to keep.

Some went out on the seventh day to gather and found none. The LORD: how long do you refuse My commandments? See, the LORD has given you the sabbath; therefore He gives bread for two days on the sixth. Stay in your place. So the people rested. Guidance: Even rest is something we have to be taught. Some will keep trying to work the very day God said sit still. Practice the gift.

The house of Israel called its name Manna; it was like coriander seed, white, taste like wafers with honey. Moses: this is what the LORD commands — fill an omer to be kept for your generations, that they may see the bread He fed you in the wilderness. Guidance: God commands a jar of manna to be saved as a memorial. The next generation needs to see, not just hear, the bread their ancestors ate.

The house of Israel called its name Manna; it was like coriander seed, white, taste like wafers with honey. Moses: this is what the LORD commands — fill an omer to be kept for your generations, that they may see the bread He fed you in the wilderness. Guidance: God commands a jar of manna to be saved as a memorial. The next generation needs to see, not just hear, the bread their ancestors ate.

Moses to Aaron: take a pot, put an omer of manna in it, and lay it up before the LORD, to be kept for your generations. So Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept, as the LORD commanded. Guidance: Memorials of God's faithfulness belong before His face. The jar of manna will sit beside the tablets of the law — daily provision and divine command together.

Moses to Aaron: take a pot, put an omer of manna in it, and lay it up before the LORD, to be kept for your generations. So Aaron laid it up before the Testimony, to be kept, as the LORD commanded. Guidance: Memorials of God's faithfulness belong before His face. The jar of manna will sit beside the tablets of the law — daily provision and divine command together.

The children of Israel ate manna forty years, until they came to a land inhabited; they ate manna until they came to the borders of Canaan. An omer is the tenth part of an ephah. Guidance: Forty years of the same bread. God can sustain you for a generation with one repeating mercy. Faithfulness in the wilderness is a long obedience eaten one morning at a time.
